title: carex neglecta
intro: Carex neglecta, also known as Woodland Sedge, is a type of perennial plant in the sedge family (Cyperaceae) that is commonly found in woodlands, wetlands, and along streams. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The health benefits of Carex neglecta include its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe skin irritations, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of Woodland Sedge can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures for topical or internal use, although its usage should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
